message:
docs: update feature parity status and add limitation tracking IDs

Downgrade Syntax, Markdown, and JSON renderable parity status from
"Implemented" to "Partial" with specific gap references:

FEATURE_PARITY.md:
- Syntax: partial (bd-2tch: custom theme/syntax loading)
- Markdown: partial (bd-3nr3: fenced code blocks, bd-3d0j: OSC8 hyperlinks)
- JSON: partial (bd-2zpy: Python Rich options parity)

renderables/markdown.rs:
- Document image rendering gap vs Python Rich OSC8 (bd-3d0j)
- Document fenced code block syntax highlighting gap (bd-3nr3)
- Add hyperlink URL suffix vs OSC8 limitation (bd-3d0j)
- Clarify footnote rendering differences

renderables/json.rs:
- Reference bd-2zpy for compact output and options parity

renderables/syntax.rs:
- Reference bd-2tch for theme and syntax definition loading gaps

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -64,9 +64,9 @@ This document is the source of truth. If code or docs change, update this file,
 | Align | Alignment | Implemented | `src/renderables/align.rs` | Golden tests |
 | Pretty / Inspect | Debug-based pretty printing + type inspection | Implemented | `src/renderables/pretty.rs` | Unit tests + snapshots in `src/renderables/snapshots/` |
 | Traceback | Traceback rendering + `Console::print_exception` | Implemented | `src/renderables/traceback.rs` | Deterministic explicit frames + Python fixture conformance (`traceback/basic`). Code context via `extra_lines` + `source_context` (from file or embedded). Automatic Rust backtrace capture via `Traceback::capture()` (requires `backtrace` feature). Locals rendering supported when provided explicitly (`TracebackFrame::locals`, `Traceback::show_locals`). |
-| Syntax | Syntax highlighting | Implemented | `src/renderables/syntax.rs` (feature `syntax`) | Python fixture conformance (`syntax/basic`, `syntax/no_terminal`). ANSI output differs (syntect vs Pygments); plain text parity verified. Line numbers, indent guides, and whitespace-preserving wrap are supported. |
-| Markdown | Markdown rendering | Implemented | `src/renderables/markdown.rs` (feature `markdown`) | Python fixture conformance (`markdown/plain`, `markdown/emphasis_no_terminal`). Both plain and ANSI parity verified. |
-| JSON | JSON pretty-print | Implemented | `src/renderables/json.rs` (feature `json`) | Python fixture conformance (`json/basic`, `json/nested`). Both plain and ANSI parity verified. |
+| Syntax | Syntax highlighting | Partial | `src/renderables/syntax.rs` (feature `syntax`) | Python fixture conformance (`syntax/basic`, `syntax/no_terminal`) covers plain layout parity. Known gaps tracked in `bd-2tch`: custom syntect theme/syntax loading (Rust-side extension for parity with Python's extensible highlighting ecosystem). |
+| Markdown | Markdown rendering | Partial | `src/renderables/markdown.rs` (feature `markdown`) | Python fixture conformance currently covers only `markdown/plain` and `markdown/emphasis_no_terminal`. Known parity gaps tracked in: `bd-3nr3` (fenced code blocks should render via `Syntax` when language is present and `syntax` feature enabled), `bd-3d0j` (links/images should render as OSC8 hyperlinks without URL suffix, matching Python Rich defaults). |
+| JSON | JSON pretty-print | Partial | `src/renderables/json.rs` (feature `json`) | Python fixture conformance covers `json/basic`, `json/nested`. Known parity gaps tracked in `bd-2zpy`: Python Rich JSON options/constructors (e.g., `indent: None|int|str`, `ensure_ascii`, `from_data`, `sort_keys`, etc.). |
 
 ---
